title: HJ vs SB: Who Owns the Flop?

scenes.headline: Flop Ownership: The Truth
scenes.voiceover: Think flops are all about aggression? In a 3-bet pot, it's all about knowing when to hold back.

scenes.headline: Preflop Powerhouse
scenes.voiceover: The Small Blind raises 19% of hands preflop, setting up for high-stakes battles. This range is stacked with premium pairs and powerful Broadway cards.

scenes.headline: Optimal Decision Tree
scenes.voiceover: In this 3-bet pot, flops decide control between strength and caution. When the top card is ill-fitted, checking emerges at 35% frequency. With flops low, like a Jack or under, it's a pivotal decision node. On low wet boards, checks spike to a cautious 44% among small bets. When the flop goes monotone, it's another decisive node moment. A monotone board shows us balance: small bets blend with a 45% check.

nodes.label: Flush Draw or Rainbow
nodes.summary: Decision branch: Flush Draw or Rainbow

When the top card is ill-fitted, checking emerges at 35% frequency.
With flops low, like a Jack or under, it's a pivotal decision node.
On low wet boards, checks spike to a cautious 44% among small bets.
When the flop goes monotone, it's another decisive node moment.
A monotone board shows us balance: small bets blend with a 45% check.

scenes.headline: Board Texture Mastery
scenes.voiceover: On Queen-high boards, the Small Blind seizes 78% aggressive action, while a deuce demands caution at just 51%. Understanding these board textures is your key edge.

scenes.headline: Master the 3-Bet Flop
scenes.voiceover: Dive into GTOCentral to master every angle of the HJ vs SB 3-bet pot. Learn how to exploit every board texture.
